subject: DevOps
title: Securing CI/CD Pipelines
description: A fast breakdown of Securing CI/CD Pipelines for builders.

hook.text: Attackers compromised a CI server and injected a backdoor. Infected binaries shipped for three weeks.

buildup.text: CI/CD has broad access: source code, secrets, cloud creds, deploy targets. It's a high-value target.

discovery.text: Harden CI/CD: ephemeral agents, limited secret access, pinned action versions, and audited configs.

twist.text: Third-party actions are supply chain risks. Pin GitHub Actions to commit SHAs, not mutable tags.

climax.text: SLSA defines build security levels. Level 3 requires isolated, hermetic, and auditable builds.

punchline.text: Your pipeline is a target. Harden it like production.

quiz.question: Why should third-party CI/CD actions be pinned to commit SHAs?
options: To prevent a compromised tag from silently changing the action code
To make the pipeline run faster
SHAs are easier to read than version tags
